mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect sustained economic growth that supports corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or earnings revisions decouple from GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 240, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or inflation fails to trigger rate hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside declining unemployment rates indicate a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when wage growth outpaces productivity, or forward returns turn negative despite improving employment metrics.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during credit expansion phases.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns when mortgage rates exceed 4.5 percent, or housing market decouples from broader equity performance.
